description.zh:
French terry is a knitted cotton fabric featuring a smooth outer surface and looped interior construction. Unlike brushed fleece, French terry provides improved breathability, lighter softness, and a more structured drape, making it highly popular in oversized streetwear hoodies and luxury casualwear. 

Heavyweight French terry fabric is engineered using loopback knitting techniques that create a textured inner surface while maintaining a smooth exterior. This construction improves airflow, moisture balance, and shape retention for premium oversized garments.
description.en:
French terry is a knitted cotton fabric featuring a smooth outer surface and looped interior construction. Unlike brushed fleece, French terry provides improved breathability, lighter softness, and a more structured drape, making it highly popular in oversized streetwear hoodies and luxury casualwear. 

Heavyweight French terry fabric is engineered using loopback knitting techniques that create a textured inner surface while maintaining a smooth exterior. This construction improves airflow, moisture balance, and shape retention for premium oversized garments.

title.zh: Why Streetwear Brands Prefer French Terry
title.en: Why Streetwear Brands Prefer French Terry

description.zh:
Many premium streetwear brands choose French terry fabric for oversized hoodies because it creates a cleaner silhouette, improved drape, and a more structured luxury feel without excessive bulk.

French terry has become a core fabric in luxury streetwear due to its ability to combine heavyweight structure with breathable comfort, making it ideal for oversized silhouettes, garment dye treatments, and vintage wash finishes.
description.en:
Many premium streetwear brands choose French terry fabric for oversized hoodies because it creates a cleaner silhouette, improved drape, and a more structured luxury feel without excessive bulk.

French terry has become a core fabric in luxury streetwear due to its ability to combine heavyweight structure with breathable comfort, making it ideal for oversized silhouettes, garment dye treatments, and vintage wash finishes.

description.zh:
Compared to brushed fleece, French terry fabric offers better breathability and a cleaner oversized drape, while fleece provides additional warmth and softness. Both fabrics are widely used in heavyweight streetwear hoodies depending on the desired fit and finish.

French terry is often preferred for premium oversized hoodies because it feels less bulky than fleece while maintaining structure and shape retention for modern streetwear silhouettes.

description.zh:
Our heavyweight French terry fabrics are developed using compact cotton yarns and high-density knitting techniques to improve durability, reduce shrinkage, and enhance fabric recovery after washing.

French terry fabric works exceptionally well with garment dye and vintage wash treatments due to its textured loopback interior and stable heavyweight construction.
description.en:
Our heavyweight French terry fabrics are developed using compact cotton yarns and high-density knitting techniques to improve durability, reduce shrinkage, and enhance fabric recovery after washing.

French terry fabric works exceptionally well with garment dye and vintage wash treatments due to its textured loopback interior and stable heavyweight construction.

description.zh:
Heavyweight French terry fabrics may shrink after washing if the yarn tension and pre-shrinking process are not properly controlled. Compact cotton and enzyme washing help improve dimensional stability.

Poor-quality French terry fabrics often lose structure after washing, resulting in weak drape and distorted oversized silhouettes. Dense loopback construction improves shape retention and garment stability.
description.en:
Heavyweight French terry fabrics may shrink after washing if the yarn tension and pre-shrinking process are not properly controlled. Compact cotton and enzyme washing help improve dimensional stability.

Poor-quality French terry fabrics often lose structure after washing, resulting in weak drape and distorted oversized silhouettes. Dense loopback construction improves shape retention and garment stability.

Oversized French terry hoodies are widely used in Los Angeles streetwear collections because of their relaxed drape, heavyweight feel, and compatibility with vintage garment dye techniques.

title.zh: The Core Characteristics of The Special Fabric
title.en: The Core Characteristics of The Special Fabric

description.zh:
Oversized t-shirt fabrics are designed with higher GSM cotton construction to create a structured boxy silhouette, improved drape, and long-lasting shape retention. Unlike standard lightweight jersey fabrics, these materials prioritize density, stability, and premium streetwear aesthetics.

Common characteristics include:
- Heavyweight cotton (280gsm–380gsm)
- Compact yarn construction for stability
- Slightly dry or structured hand feel
- Minimal fabric twist after washing
description.en:
Oversized t-shirt fabrics are designed with higher GSM cotton construction to create a structured boxy silhouette, improved drape, and long-lasting shape retention. Unlike standard lightweight jersey fabrics, these materials prioritize density, stability, and premium streetwear aesthetics.

Common characteristics include:
- Heavyweight cotton (280gsm–380gsm)
- Compact yarn construction for stability
- Slightly dry or structured hand feel
- Minimal fabric twist after washing

title.zh: Why must be made of this particular fabric
title.en: Why must be made of this particular fabric

description.zh:
Oversized t-shirts require structured fabrics to maintain their intended silhouette. Lightweight cotton tends to collapse or cling to the body, which destroys the boxy streetwear look.

Heavyweight cotton fabrics are used because they:
- Support oversized silhouettes without deformation
- Enhance garment drape for a luxury streetwear feel
- Provide a more substantial and premium wearing experience
- Improve visual balance in drop-shoulder designs
description.en:
Oversized t-shirts require structured fabrics to maintain their intended silhouette. Lightweight cotton tends to collapse or cling to the body, which destroys the boxy streetwear look.

Heavyweight cotton fabrics are used because they:
- Support oversized silhouettes without deformation
- Enhance garment drape for a luxury streetwear feel
- Provide a more substantial and premium wearing experience
- Improve visual balance in drop-shoulder designs

title.zh: Analysis of Fabric Shrinkage Rate and Stability
title.en: Analysis of Fabric Shrinkage Rate and Stability

description.zh:
Shrinkage control is a critical factor in oversized t-shirt production. Standard untreated cotton fabrics may shrink between 3%–8% after washing, which affects fit consistency.

For premium streetwear production, stabilized cotton fabrics are used with:
- Pre-shrunk treatment
- Enzyme washing process
- Compact spinning yarn technology

Final shrinkage can be controlled within 1.5%–3%, ensuring consistent oversized fit across production batches.
description.en:
Shrinkage control is a critical factor in oversized t-shirt production. Standard untreated cotton fabrics may shrink between 3%–8% after washing, which affects fit consistency.

For premium streetwear production, stabilized cotton fabrics are used with:
- Pre-shrunk treatment
- Enzyme washing process
- Compact spinning yarn technology

Final shrinkage can be controlled within 1.5%–3%, ensuring consistent oversized fit across production batches.

title.zh: Why are these fabrics suitable for Streetwear
title.en: Why are these fabrics suitable for Streetwear

description.zh:
Heavyweight oversized t-shirt fabrics are widely adopted by streetwear brands due to their ability to combine structure, durability, and aesthetic consistency.

These fabrics are ideal for:
- Boxy fit streetwear collections
- Vintage washed t-shirts
- Luxury blank essentials
- Drop shoulder oversized silhouettes

The combination of GSM control and cotton density ensures long-term shape retention and a premium streetwear appearance even after repeated washing.

subtitle.zh: 在这里您可以找到常见问题的解答
subtitle.en: FAQ - Find answers to common questions here

question.zh: Q: What fabric is best for oversized t-shirts?
question.en: Q: What fabric is best for oversized t-shirts?

answer.zh: A: Heavyweight cotton fabrics between 280gsm and 380gsm are commonly used for oversized t-shirts because they provide better structure, improved drape, and long-lasting shape retention compared to lightweight jersey fabrics.
answer.en: A: Heavyweight cotton fabrics between 280gsm and 380gsm are commonly used for oversized t-shirts because they provide better structure, improved drape, and long-lasting shape retention compared to lightweight jersey fabrics.

question.zh: Q: Why do oversized t-shirts use heavyweight cotton?
question.en: Q: Why do oversized t-shirts use heavyweight cotton?

answer.zh: A: Heavyweight cotton helps oversized t-shirts maintain a boxy silhouette and premium streetwear appearance. Lighter fabrics often collapse or lose structure after washing, while heavyweight fabrics create a cleaner and more balanced fit.
answer.en: A: Heavyweight cotton helps oversized t-shirts maintain a boxy silhouette and premium streetwear appearance. Lighter fabrics often collapse or lose structure after washing, while heavyweight fabrics create a cleaner and more balanced fit.

question.zh: Q: What GSM is recommended for streetwear oversized tees?
question.en: Q: What GSM is recommended for streetwear oversized tees?

answer.zh: A: Most premium streetwear brands use 300gsm–350gsm cotton for oversized t-shirts. This weight range offers a balance between structure, comfort, durability, and oversized drape.
answer.en: A: Most premium streetwear brands use 300gsm–350gsm cotton for oversized t-shirts. This weight range offers a balance between structure, comfort, durability, and oversized drape.

question.zh: Q: Do heavyweight oversized t-shirts shrink after washing?
question.en: Q: Do heavyweight oversized t-shirts shrink after washing?

answer.zh: A: Shrinkage depends on fabric treatment and yarn construction. Pre-shrunk heavyweight cotton fabrics typically maintain shrinkage within 1.5%–3% after washing, helping preserve oversized fit consistency.
answer.en: A: Shrinkage depends on fabric treatment and yarn construction. Pre-shrunk heavyweight cotton fabrics typically maintain shrinkage within 1.5%–3% after washing, helping preserve oversized fit consistency.

question.zh: Q: What is the difference between regular cotton and compact cotton?
question.en: Q: What is the difference between regular cotton and compact cotton?

answer.zh: A: Compact cotton uses tighter yarn spinning technology to reduce hairiness, improve fabric density, and enhance shape retention. It is commonly used in premium oversized streetwear garments for a cleaner and more stable finish.
answer.en: A: Compact cotton uses tighter yarn spinning technology to reduce hairiness, improve fabric density, and enhance shape retention. It is commonly used in premium oversized streetwear garments for a cleaner and more stable finish.

question.zh: Q: Why do some oversized t-shirts lose shape easily?
question.en: Q: Why do some oversized t-shirts lose shape easily?

answer.zh: A: Oversized t-shirts may lose shape when low-density cotton fabrics or unstable knitting structures are used. Heavyweight compact cotton fabrics help improve garment stability and reduce deformation after repeated washing.
answer.en: A: Oversized t-shirts may lose shape when low-density cotton fabrics or unstable knitting structures are used. Heavyweight compact cotton fabrics help improve garment stability and reduce deformation after repeated washing.

question.zh: Q: What colors are most popular for oversized streetwear t-shirts?
question.en: Q: What colors are most popular for oversized streetwear t-shirts?

answer.zh: A: Washed black, vintage grey, cream, olive green, and earth-tone colors are widely used in modern streetwear collections because they complement oversized silhouettes and vintage-inspired aesthetics.
answer.en: A: Washed black, vintage grey, cream, olive green, and earth-tone colors are widely used in modern streetwear collections because they complement oversized silhouettes and vintage-inspired aesthetics.

question.zh: Q: Are garment dye fabrics suitable for oversized t-shirts?
question.en: Q: Are garment dye fabrics suitable for oversized t-shirts?

answer.zh: A: Yes. Garment dye fabrics are highly popular in oversized streetwear because they create vintage fade effects, softer textures, and more unique color depth compared to standard reactive dye fabrics.
answer.en: A: Yes. Garment dye fabrics are highly popular in oversized streetwear because they create vintage fade effects, softer textures, and more unique color depth compared to standard reactive dye fabrics.

question.zh: Q: What fabric finish is best for premium streetwear tees?
question.en: Q: What fabric finish is best for premium streetwear tees?

answer.zh: A: Enzyme wash, garment dye, and vintage wash finishes are commonly used in premium streetwear t-shirts because they improve softness, enhance texture, and create a worn-in luxury appearance.
answer.en: A: Enzyme wash, garment dye, and vintage wash finishes are commonly used in premium streetwear t-shirts because they improve softness, enhance texture, and create a worn-in luxury appearance.

question.zh: Q: Why do luxury oversized t-shirts feel heavier?
question.en: Q: Why do luxury oversized t-shirts feel heavier?

answer.zh: A: Luxury oversized t-shirts typically use higher GSM cotton fabrics with denser knitting structures, resulting in improved drape, durability, and a more substantial premium feel.
answer.en: A: Luxury oversized t-shirts typically use higher GSM cotton fabrics with denser knitting structures, resulting in improved drape, durability, and a more substantial premium feel.

question.zh: Q: Is heavyweight cotton too hot for oversized t-shirts?
question.en: Q: Is heavyweight cotton too hot for oversized t-shirts?

answer.zh: A: Not necessarily. High-quality heavyweight cotton fabrics are designed to balance density and breathability, allowing oversized t-shirts to maintain structure while remaining comfortable for everyday wear.
answer.en: A: Not necessarily. High-quality heavyweight cotton fabrics are designed to balance density and breathability, allowing oversized t-shirts to maintain structure while remaining comfortable for everyday wear.

question.zh: Q: What makes a premium oversized t-shirt fabric different?
question.en: Q: What makes a premium oversized t-shirt fabric different?

answer.zh: A: Premium oversized t-shirt fabrics focus on fabric density, shape retention, cotton quality, and surface texture. These elements help achieve a cleaner boxy fit and long-lasting streetwear silhouette.
answer.en: A: Premium oversized t-shirt fabrics focus on fabric density, shape retention, cotton quality, and surface texture. These elements help achieve a cleaner boxy fit and long-lasting streetwear silhouette.

question.zh: Q: Which fabric is best for boxy fit t-shirts?
question.en: Q: Which fabric is best for boxy fit t-shirts?

answer.zh: A: Heavyweight cotton jersey and compact cotton fabrics are commonly used for boxy fit t-shirts because they provide structure, stability, and improved shoulder drape.
answer.en: A: Heavyweight cotton jersey and compact cotton fabrics are commonly used for boxy fit t-shirts because they provide structure, stability, and improved shoulder drape.

question.zh: Q: Why are washed colors popular in streetwear?
question.en: Q: Why are washed colors popular in streetwear?

answer.zh: A: Washed colors create a vintage aesthetic and worn-in texture that complement oversized streetwear silhouettes. They also add depth and softness to heavyweight cotton garments.
answer.en: A: Washed colors create a vintage aesthetic and worn-in texture that complement oversized streetwear silhouettes. They also add depth and softness to heavyweight cotton garments.

question.zh: Q: Can heavyweight oversized t-shirts be used for luxury streetwear brands?
question.en: Q: Can heavyweight oversized t-shirts be used for luxury streetwear brands?

answer.zh: A: Yes. Heavyweight oversized t-shirt fabrics are widely used by luxury and boutique streetwear brands because they support premium silhouettes, structured drape, and elevated garment finishes.
answer.en: A: Yes. Heavyweight oversized t-shirt fabrics are widely used by luxury and boutique streetwear brands because they support premium silhouettes, structured drape, and elevated garment finishes.
